name: Virginia Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 9 x 13-inch baking pan; set aside.
2. In a heavy 4-quart saucepan, combine milk, butter, corn syrup, granulated sugar and brown sugar. Place over medium heat and stir occasionally with a wooden spoon until mixture comes to a boil.
3. Clip on candy thermometer. Cook to 234 F (115 C) or soft-ball stage.
4. Pour the mixture into the baking pan without scraping; let cool to lukewarm.
5. Add vanilla and stir with a wooden spoon until the mixture thickens.
6. Add nuts and continue stirring until the candy loses its gloss.
7. Scrape out onto plastic wrap. Pat into a loaf shape (about 9" x 5"). Slice and serve, or wrap in plastic and store in the refrigerator for several weeks.
Makes about 50 slices or 75 pieces.
VARIATION: Use 1 teaspoon maple extract in place of the vanilla.
